structured.title: Family Responds To Mum’s Fall At Home
structured.overview: Emily speaks on the phone with a family member about their mum having a fall at home. Omi, Paul, and Kate are already with mum, but they are struggling to lift her and dad is not answering his phone. Emily agrees to go over as quickly as possible to help. Another person gives clear guidance on how to move mum safely: avoid lifting her directly from the floor to standing, instead position a chair behind her, raise her gradually with cushions, and scoot her toward the chair. There is some concern about the children present being stressed, and Emily searches for her keys to leave immediately.

action_items.description: Go to mum’s house to help lift her after her fall

apps_results.content:
## Initial call and concern
- Emily contacted mom asking if she’s okay  
- Mom said she’s in a tricky position, needs help  
- Emily confirmed she’s free to talk and help  

## Situation with mom’s fall
- Mom has had a fall at home  
- Omi, Paul, and Paul’s partner Kate are present  
- Dad not answering phone despite calls  

## Plan to go and help
- Emily decides to come down as quickly as possible  
- She may also call dad again on the way  
- Family needs extra person to help lift mom  

## Safe way to move mom
- Do not lift mom straight from floor to standing  
- Place a chair behind her and move her onto it  
- Use cushions to raise her gradually, then scoot to chair  

## Household context and stress
- Kids are present and likely stressed by situation  
- No time to organize wider family group chat  
- Emily searching for keys, sounding breathless/husky  

## Next steps
- Emily travels to mom’s house immediately  
- Family follows safe lifting method with chair and cushions  
- Continue trying to reach dad while managing situation
